title: The Third Hour
story_reference: Based on story-004-the-question-that-broke-everything.md, scene: hour three of asking 'Who am I?' — the moment the noise thins
hook: He had been asking the question for three hours. The clever answers dried up after the first.

script.visual: Predawn. A riverbank. The disciple sits cross-legged, alone. Breath visible in the cold. His face is tight with frustration. One instruction given.
script.voiceover: One instruction. 'Sit by the river. Ask yourself — who am I? Do not stop asking until the answer comes.'

script.visual: Close on his face. Time-lapse feel. His mouth shapes the question silently, over and over.
script.voiceover: Hour one. The clever answers came first. 'I am the awareness behind the five sheaths.' 'I am Brahman.' 'I am the unchanging witness.' The words were correct. They did not land.

script.visual: Quick memory flashes, each held a half-second: a father's cold glance across a dinner table; a boardroom with six silent faces; a child in a doorway asking something he does not answer. The memories are involuntary, unwanted.
script.voiceover: Hour two. The memories came. His father's appraising eyes. The first time the board looked at him like a stranger. His daughter, in a doorway: 'Daddy, why are you always somewhere else?'

script.visual: Back to the riverbank. His body in pain. Legs aching. Back burning. His mouth twists. He opens his eyes, looks at the river, considers leaving. Does not.
script.voiceover: Hour three. The memories gave way to noise. His legs ached. His mind screamed for a phone, a book, anything. He opened his eyes. He thought about standing up. He stayed.

script.visual: Very slow push-in on his face. The tightness loosens. Not peace — exhaustion. A hollowness. The sky behind him lightens by one shade.
script.voiceover: And then the noise began to thin. Not because he had conquered it. Because he had run out of fuel.

script.visual: Extreme close on his eyes. They are open. Something behind them — subtle — has noticed itself. The river runs gold. A single bird begins to sing, badly.
script.voiceover: In the hollowness, something moved. Not a thought. Not a feeling. Something prior to both. The awareness of awareness itself. It had always been there. He had been looking past it his whole life.

script.visual: The Guru, who we did not know was behind him, sits down quietly beside him. Neither speaks. Cut to black.
script.voiceover: 'You did not find an answer,' the Guru said. 'You found the one who was asking.'

cta: Scene five. One question. Three hours. No answer — and that is how it works.
caption:
He was given one instruction. 'Ask yourself — who am I? Do not stop until the answer comes.'

For three hours the clever answers came and did not land. Then the memories. Then the noise. Then the ache in the legs and the urge to leave.

Then the noise thinned — not because he conquered it, but because he ran out of fuel.

In the hollowness, something noticed itself.

The Vivekachudamani calls this the highest devotion. Not prayer. Not ritual. The continuous inquiry into one's own true nature — even when it produces nothing but silence.

ātma-vicāra
music_suggestion: Silence for the first 16 seconds except for cold wind and distant water. A single piano note enters every 8 seconds during the memory flashes — sparse, never a melody. At the moment the noise thins (38s), the piano stops. Only river and one bird. No swell. No resolution. The reel ends in near-silence.
casting_notes: One character on screen for most of the reel: The Seeker, sitting in genuine cold. The actor must be able to hold a 30-second face that changes by micro-expression only — frustration to exhaustion to noticing. Cast for the face, not the body. The Guru appears only in the final three seconds, seated, not speaking more than one line. The memory flashes (father, boardroom, daughter) should be shot as separate inserts — a single-second gesture is enough for each. If actors are unavailable, this reel can be shot entirely as close-ups of one face in cold light with voiceover carrying the full narrative.
